Control Arm.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' This clearly identifies 'ADT with bicalutamide' as the control arm.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)." This indicates two arms: one experimental and one control.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: On page 1, under the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'In this open-label randomized phase III study, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' The experimental arm is Treatment Arm 1.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis, 638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm and 641 to the control arm.' This is further confirmed in Table 1, where 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' is listed, and in Figure 1 and Figure A1, which show 'LHRHa plus bicalutamide' (control arm) with N=641 for the ITT analysis population.

Treatment Arm(s).value: ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily)
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)." The experimental arm is explicitly named here.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: On page 1, in the 'PURPOSE' section, it states: 'Orteronel (TAK-700) is a nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor suppressing androgen synthesis.' The 'METHODS' section on page 1 identifies 'ADT with orteronel' as the experimental arm.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS'. This is confirmed in the 'RESULTS -> Patients' section on page 4: 'the median follow-up time was 4.9 years.' Converting 4.9 years to months (4.9 * 12) gives 58.8 months.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis, 638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm and 641 to the control arm.' The ADT plus orteronel arm is identified as the experimental arm in the 'METHODS' section on the same page. This is further supported by Table 1 on page 5, which lists 'Orteronel (n = 638)' as the experimental arm.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document describes primary and secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) response rates, and adverse events (AEs). There is no mention of a 'Quality of Life Scale' or any assessment of participants' overall well-being or satisfaction with their health status throughout the abstract, methods, results, or discussion sections, nor in any of the tables or figures.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document was reviewed for mentions of 'Quality of Life' or related terms in the abstract, methods, endpoints, results, and discussion sections, as well as in tables and figures. No information regarding the quality of life of participants was found.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis...'. On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'Patients', it further clarifies: '...1,279 were included in the intention-to-treat analysis'. Table 1 on page 5 is titled 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis (N = 1,279)', confirming this number as the total participants for the primary analysis.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports median progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) for the treatment arm (Orteronel), but not the median duration participants were actually on-treatment. This specific metric was not found.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document reports median progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) for the control arm (Bicalutamide), but not the median duration participants were actually on-treatment. This specific metric was not found.

Trial.evidence: The full name of the clinical study, including its identifier, is provided in the title of the document on page 1.

NCT.evidence: Found under "CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION" on page 8.

PubMed ID.evidence: The document does not explicitly state a PubMed ID.

Author.evidence: Extracted from the author list on page 1, using "et al." for brevity as allowed.

Year.evidence: Found on page 1, indicating the publication year as "published at ascopubs.org/journal/jco on April 21, 2022".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains extensive sections including methods, results, discussion, and multiple pages of content, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract. An "abstract" section is present, but it's part of a larger full publication.

Phase.evidence: Explicitly stated as "Phase III Trial" in the title on page 1 and confirmed in the "Key Objective" and "Trial Design and Conduct" sections on page 2.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document is presented as an "original report" on page 1 and describes the primary findings of the SWOG-1216 trial, indicating it is an original publication of the trial results.

'Extensive' disease is defined as high volume disease on page 2.

'Extensive' disease is defined as high volume disease on page 2.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in TABLE 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis (N = 1,279)', under 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column, row 'Disease severity, No. (%) - Minimal'. 'Minimal' disease is defined as low volume disease on page 2.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: On page 5, in TABLE 1. Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis, under the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column (control arm), the 'Median age, years (range)' is reported as '68.1 (19.4-92.3)'.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis', row 'Bone metastases, No. (%)', under the 'Orteronel (n = 638)' column, 'Yes' entry. The study is for 'newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer', implying baseline metastases are synchronous.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The document describes progression events but does not provide a specific breakdown of the number and percentage of participants with metachronous metastases (metastases that occur after diagnosis) for the treatment arm.

The document describes progression events but does not provide a specific breakdown of the number and percentage of participants with metachronous metastases (metastases that occur after diagnosis) for the control arm.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study population is defined as 'newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' (Page 1, Purpose), which inherently means synchronous metastases. The study does not report outcomes by prognostic groups comparing synchronous versus metachronous disease, as the entire cohort falls under the synchronous definition.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The study focuses on 'newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' (Page 1, Purpose), implying synchronous disease. There is no mention of metachronous disease or reporting of results based on prognostic groups with metachronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states that 'Patient assignment was dynamically balanced on three stratification factors: severity of disease (minimal v extensive)'. Figure 3 on page 6 and Table A3 on page 13 report outcomes by 'Extensive Disease' (n = 656 and n = 310/313 respectively), which is defined as 'greater than minimal involvement' (Page 2) and clarified in Table A2 footnote 'b' as 'extensive (high) risk'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states that 'Patient assignment was dynamically balanced on three stratification factors: severity of disease (minimal v extensive)'. Figure 3 on page 6 and Table A3 on page 13 report outcomes by 'Minimal Disease' (n = 623 and n = 328/328 respectively), which is defined as 'involvement of vertebrae and/or pelvic bones and/or lymph nodes' (Page 2) and clarified in Table A2 footnote 'b' as 'minimal disease'.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'abstract' section under 'METHODS', it states: 'The primary objective was the comparison of overall survival (OS)'. On page 3, under the 'End Points' section, it explicitly states: 'The primary end point was OS, which was defined as the time from random assignment to the date of death from any cause.'

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Found in TABLE 1, under 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' row '0', in the 'Orteronel (n = 638)' column on page 5.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Found in TABLE 1, under 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' row '0', in the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column on page 5.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Derived from TABLE 1 on page 5, by summing the values for 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' rows '1' (175 (27%)) and '2' (21 (3%)) in the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column. (175+21 = 196; 27%+3% = 30%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Control.evidence: Found in TABLE 1, 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis, No. (%)' section, under 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column. Summed values for '< 7' (39 (6%)) and '7' (168 (26%)) to get 207 (32%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Control.evidence: Found in TABLE 1, 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis, No. (%)' section, under 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column. Summed values for '8' (118 (19%)) and '9-10' (264 (41%)) to get 382 (60%).

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The document states that 'No other prior systemic therapy for metastatic prostate cancer was allowed (with the exception of up to 30 days of ADT for metastatic disease before registration)' on page 2. The assigned treatment for the experimental arm was ADT with orteronel. Docetaxel was not part of the assigned treatment. Furthermore, patients were eligible if they were 'unsuitable candidates for docetaxel or if they declined docetaxel therapy' (page 2). Docetaxel is mentioned as a 'subsequent life-prolonging therapy' received after discontinuation of protocol therapy, not before or during the trial as part of the assigned treatment (page 6). Therefore, the number and percentage of participants who received docetaxel before or during the trial in the treatment arm is not reported.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The document states that 'No other prior systemic therapy for metastatic prostate cancer was allowed (with the exception of up to 30 days of ADT for metastatic disease before registration)' on page 2. The assigned treatment for the control arm was ADT with bicalutamide. Docetaxel was not part of the assigned treatment. Furthermore, patients were eligible if they were 'unsuitable candidates for docetaxel or if they declined docetaxel therapy' (page 2). Docetaxel is mentioned as a 'subsequent life-prolonging therapy' received after discontinuation of protocol therapy, not before or during the trial as part of the assigned treatment (page 6). Therefore, the number and percentage of participants who received docetaxel before or during the trial in the control arm is not reported.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months (≤ 0.2 v0.2 to ≤ 4 v > 4 ng/mL), and adverse event profile. More specifically, secondary end points were PFS, PSA response rates (complete response [CR; PSA < 0.2 ng/mL], partial response [PR; PSA between 0.2 and 4.0 ng/mL], and no response [NR; PSA > 4.0 ng/mL] at a 7-month landmark after random assignment), and adverse events (AEs) rate.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'Secondary end points were progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months (≤ 0.2 v0.2 to ≤ 4 v > 4 ng/mL), and adverse event profile.' On page 3, in the 'End Points' section, it further elaborates: 'Secondary end points were PFS, PSA response rates, and adverse events (AEs) rate. PFS was defined as the time from random assignment to first documentation of PSA progression... PSA response rates were divided into complete response (CR; PSA < 0.2 ng/mL), partial response (PR; PSA between 0.2 and 4.0 ng/mL), and no response (NR; PSA > 4.0 ng/mL) at a 7-month landmark after random assignment.'

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' This indicates that orteronel and bicalutamide are the additional therapies given in combination with androgen deprivation therapy (ADT).

Type of Therapy.value: androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with orteronel or ADT with bicalutamide
Type of Therapy.evidence: On page 1, under the 'PURPOSE' section, it states: 'We evaluated the clinical benefit of orteronel when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) in patients with newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.' In the 'METHODS' section, it further specifies: 'patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' This describes the two types of treatment provided.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.value: The lack of correlation of PFS and PSA response with OS raises concerns over assumption of their consistent surrogacy for OS in the context of extensive postprotocol therapy in this setting.
COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The value is extracted from the 'CONCLUSION' section on page 1, which states: 'The study did not meet the primary end point of improved OS with orteronel. The lack of correlation of PFS and PSA response with OS raises concerns over assumption of their consistent surrogacy for OS in the context of extensive postprotocol therapy in this setting.' This directly addresses the risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (PFS and PSA response) as surrogates for OS.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'but not in OS (median 81.1 v 70.2 months...'. The 81.1 months refers to the orteronel arm (treatment). This is confirmed on page 3, in 'FIG 1. Intention-to-treat comparison of overall survival by arm', which shows 'LHRH agonist plus orteronel: median 81.1 months'.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'but not in OS (median 81.1 v 70.2 months...'. The 70.2 months refers to the control arm. This is confirmed on page 3, in 'FIG 1. Intention-to-treat comparison of overall survival by arm', which shows 'LHRH agonist plus bicalutamide: median 70.2 months'.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: On page 13, 'TABLE A3. Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' lists 'Extensive' disease (which corresponds to high volume). For the 'Orteronel Arm', it provides 'OS Orteronel Median/5 Year' as '4.8 years/47%', which is a 5-year OS rate, not the median OS in months. The median OS in months for this subgroup in the treatment arm is not explicitly reported in the document.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: On page 13, 'TABLE A3. Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' lists 'Extensive' disease (which corresponds to high volume). For the 'Control Arm', it provides 'OS Control Median/5 Year' as '4.1 years/44%', which is a 5-year OS rate, not the median OS in months. The median OS in months for this subgroup in the control arm is not explicitly reported in the document.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: On page 13, 'TABLE A3. Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' lists 'Minimal' disease (which corresponds to low volume). For the 'Orteronel Arm', it states 'OS Orteronel Median/5 Year' as 'NR/71%'. According to the abbreviations on page 13, 'NR' means 'not reached'.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: On page 13, 'TABLE A3. Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' lists 'Minimal' disease (which corresponds to low volume). For the 'Control Arm', it states 'OS Control Median/5 Year' as 'NR/71%'. According to the abbreviations on page 13, 'NR' means 'not reached'.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.evidence: The document reports 'PSA response rates' (CR 58%; PR: 22%, NR: 19%) for the orteronel arm on page 4 under 'Secondary End Points'. However, the prompt specifically asks for 'overall response (complete or partial response) of metastatic disease', which typically refers to tumor response based on imaging (e.g., RECIST criteria), not biochemical PSA response. While the document mentions that efficacy was assessed using modified RECIST version 1.1, the actual results for ORR based on tumor response are not reported. Therefore, the requested value is not found.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | CR.evidence: The document reports 'PSA response rates' (CR 58%) for the orteronel arm on page 4 under 'Secondary End Points'. However, the prompt specifically asks for 'complete response of metastatic disease', which typically refers to tumor response based on imaging (e.g., RECIST criteria), not biochemical PSA response. While the document mentions that efficacy was assessed using modified RECIST version 1.1, the actual results for CR based on tumor response are not reported. Therefore, the requested value is not found.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | SD.evidence: The document reports 'PSA response rates' including 'NR: 19%' for the orteronel arm on page 4 under 'Secondary End Points'. 'NR' (No Response) is defined as PSA > 4.0 ng/mL, which is a biochemical measure. The prompt asks for 'stable metastatic disease', which typically refers to tumor response based on imaging (e.g., RECIST criteria). The document does not provide specific data for stable disease (SD) based on metastatic disease assessment. Therefore, the requested value is not found.

Treatment Class.evidence: The 'PURPOSE' section on page 1 states: 'We evaluated the clinical benefit of orteronel when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) in patients with newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.' The 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1 also mentions: 'The cornerstone of treatment for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC) is androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with medical or surgical castration.'

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'Primary End Point' section on page 4, stating 'The OS rate at 5 years was 59.7% in the orteronel group'. This is also supported by Figure 1 on page 3, which shows a 5-year estimate of 59% for the LHRHa plus orteronel arm.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'Primary End Point' section on page 4, stating 'and 57.9% in the control group'. This is also supported by Figure 1 on page 3, which shows a 5-year estimate of 58% for the LHRHa plus bicalutamide arm.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Treatment.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with synchronous metastasis in the treatment arm.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Control.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with synchronous metastasis in the control arm.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Treatment.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with metachronous metastasis in the treatment arm.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Control.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with metachronous metastasis in the control arm.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', 'Extensive' row, 'OS Orteronel Median/5 Year' column, which states '4.8 years/47%'. Footnote 'b' of Table A2 on page 12 defines 'extensive (high) risk' for S1216 as 'metastatic disease beyond axial skeleton and/or lymph nodes', which corresponds to high volume metastasis.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.evidence: Found in Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', 'Extensive' row, 'OS Control Median/5 Year' column, which states '4.1 years/44%'. Footnote 'b' of Table A2 on page 12 defines 'extensive (high) risk' for S1216 as 'metastatic disease beyond axial skeleton and/or lymph nodes', which corresponds to high volume metastasis.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', 'Minimal' row, 'OS Orteronel Median/5 Year' column, which states 'NR/71%'. 'Minimal' disease is considered low volume metastasis.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Found in Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', 'Minimal' row, 'OS Control Median/5 Year' column, which states 'NR/71%'. 'Minimal' disease is considered low volume metastasis.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS (median 47.6 v 23.0 months...)' and the 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4 confirms: 'The median PFS in the orteronel group was 47.6 months'. Figure 2 also shows 'LHRH agonist plus orteronel: median 47.6 months'.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS (median 47.6 v 23.0 months...)' and the 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4 confirms: '...compared with 23.0 months in the control group'. Figure 2 also shows 'LHRH agonist plus bicalutamide: median 23.0 months'.

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Treatmen.evidence: Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', shows 'Extensive' disease (which corresponds to 'High volume' as per footnote b of Table A2 on page 12). For the 'Orteronel Arm', the 'PFS Orteronel Median/5 Year' is '2.2 years'. Converting to months: 2.2 * 12 = 26.4 months.

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', shows 'Extensive' disease (which corresponds to 'High volume' as per footnote b of Table A2 on page 12). For the 'Control Arm', the 'PFS Control Median/5 Year' is '1.1 years'. Converting to months: 1.1 * 12 = 13.2 months.

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', shows 'Minimal' disease (which corresponds to 'Low volume'). For the 'Orteronel Arm', the 'PFS Orteronel Median/5 Year' is 'NR' (Not Reached), meaning the median PFS was not reported.

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Table A3 on page 13, under 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity', shows 'Minimal' disease (which corresponds to 'Low volume'). For the 'Control Arm', the 'PFS Control Median/5 Year' is '3.6 years'. Converting to months: 3.6 * 12 = 43.2 months.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The PFS rate at 4 years for the orteronel (treatment) group was 50.2%, as stated in the 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4. The hazard ratio for progression-free survival comparing orteronel to control was 0.58 (95% CI, 0.51 to 0.67; P < .001). Note: Figure 2's '4-Year Estimate' table shows 34% for LHRHa plus orteronel, which contradicts the narrative text.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The PFS rate at 4 years for the bicalutamide (control) group was 33.9%, as stated in the 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4. The hazard ratio for progression-free survival comparing orteronel to control was 0.58 (95% CI, 0.51 to 0.67; P < .001). Note: Figure 2's '4-Year Estimate' table shows 50% for LHRHa plus bicalutamide, which contradicts the narrative text.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'More grade 3/4 adverse events occurred in the experimental versus the control arms (43% v 14%).' This is confirmed in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'More grade 3 and 4 AEs occurred in the orteronel group versus the control group (43% v13%).' The experimental arm refers to the treatment arm (Orteronel).

Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'More grade 3/4 adverse events occurred in the experimental versus the control arms (43% v 14%).' This is confirmed in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'More grade 3 and 4 AEs occurred in the orteronel group versus the control group (43% v13%).' The control arm refers to the bicalutamide arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document reports 'More grade 3 and 4 AEs' (43% for treatment, 13% for control) in the 'RESULTS' and 'Safety' sections, but these are described as 'all-cause' or without explicitly specifying 'treatment-related'. While Table 2 lists individual AEs and their Grade 3-5 counts, and states 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying the listed AEs are considered related, it does not provide a total N (%) for all treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher AEs across all categories.

While Table 2 lists individual AEs and their Grade 3-5 counts, and states 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying the listed AEs are considered related, it does not provide a total N (%) for all treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher AEs across all categories.

Found in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'Five patients in the experimental arm... had grade 5 AEs'. The experimental arm is the treatment arm (Orteronel). The note below Table 2 on page 7 further details these Grade 5 AEs for the Orteronel arm. The total number of patients in the Orteronel arm for AE analysis is 627 (from Table 2 header). Percentage calculated as (5/627)*100 = 0.797%, rounded to 0.8%. The note below Table 2 also states 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying these Grade 5 AEs are considered treatment-related.

Found in the 'Safety' section on page 4: '...and one patient in the control arm had grade 5 AEs'. The control arm is the bicalutamide arm. The note below Table 2 on page 7 further details this Grade 5 AE for the bicalutamide arm. The total number of patients in the Bicalutamide arm for AE analysis is 629 (from Table 2 header). Percentage calculated as (1/629)*100 = 0.159%, rounded to 0.2%. The note below Table 2 also states 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying this Grade 5 AE is considered treatment-related.
